title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past weaknesses in using his left hand due to wrist injuries in 2021 and 2023.
Recent improvements in Brown's left hand have enhanced his gameplay, contributing to his career-high stats this season.
Brown's enhanced abilities have placed him in MVP discussions, leading the Celtics to a top-4 playoff seed.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former Red Sox announcer, returns to Boston with the San Diego Padres, the team he joined in 2016.
Orsillo reflects on his tenure at NESN, calling it his 'dream job', despite his departure 11 years ago.
Orsillo reunites with ex-colleagues Jim Rice and Tom Caron, sharing moments and memories from his Red Sox days.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora utilizes Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to defensive challenges early in the Red Sox season.
Roman Anthony's inconsistent outfield performance includes an error against the Padres, but Cora notes improvement in his throwing.
Anthony hits a triple against the Padres, contributing to the Red Sox offense, despite the team's 2-6 start to the season.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed four walks and three hits in a 3-2 loss to the Padres.
Despite control issues, Early limited the Padres to two runs in four innings, with 80 pitches in three frames.
Early's performance marked his sixth career start with 39 strikeouts, tying him with Daisuke Matsuzaka in franchise history.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0, with goals from Langoni, Fofana, and Miller, in their second home win.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received an early red card for leaving the technical area, marking his first dismissal.
Revolution center backs scored another goal from a set piece, highlighting the impact of set piece coach Marc Ortí Esteban.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas to resume batting practice after rib injury; expected back after intercostal strain recovery.
Johan Oviedo experiencing elbow issues; will consult Tommy John surgeon after reduced pitch velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval near return with upcoming rehab assignments in minor leagues.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, plans to run the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Diagnosed at 19 with aggressive thyroid cancer, Taylor attributes her recovery to Mass General's specialized treatment and care.
Taylor dedicates her marathon run to her doctors, the AYA program, and fellow cancer survivors who supported her journey.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 to support BMC's social workers who assist patients in crisis and need.
Training includes early runs and fundraising events, emphasizing the importance of community support for BMC's work.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, remaining winless in the NWSL regular season.
Head coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience and improvement, focusing on maintaining composure in the final third of the field.
Amanda Gutierres, despite her notable involvement, struggled with passing accuracy and has yet to score for Legacy FC.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended attorney from Melrose, was charged with embezzling hundreds of thousands from clients.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 meant for a client trust account and continued law practice despite a suspension.
He was arraigned in Malden Court, ordered to surrender his passport, and set with $2,500 bail.
